rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type annexes for interface behavior and implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the current component scope in AGREE annexes.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should reference only visible ports; implementation contracts may use subcomponent paths.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the analyzed model.
Assign statements in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`
Assumptions and guarantees for ports belong in component type scopes
Implementation-level bindings go in implementation annexes after relevant subcomponents
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Do not use event ports as numeric or Boolean values in AGREE expressions
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
